answers.answer: Under Article 28(1) of the General Partnership Law 2004, each Partner is jointly and severally liable with the other Partners for all debts and obligations of the General Partnership incurred while he is a Partner, unless otherwise agreed by all the other Partners.
Under the Foundations Law DIFC Law No. 3 of 2018, the maximum fine for failing to provide a copy of an assignment of rights to the Registered Agent or Registrar within 30 days is $1,500.
Under the Operating Law, the Board of Directors of the DIFCA is responsible for appointing and dismissing the Registrar, but it must consult the President prior to doing so.
The result of the application heard in case CFI 057/2025 is not explicitly stated in the provided context, as the order document is cut off before detailing the outcome.
DIFC Law No. 2 of 2022 amended the Common Reporting Standard Law (DIFC Law No. 2 of 2018) and the General Partnership Law (DIFC Law No. 11 of 2004).
ONORA
The provided context does not contain any information about the result of an application in case SCT 169/2025.

Under the Common Reporting Standard Law 2018, the effective date for due diligence requirements for Pre-existing Accounts is 31 December 2016, and for New Accounts is 1 January 2017, and these provisions apply retrospectively.
According to Article 2 of the DIFC Personal Property Law 2005, this Law was made by the Ruler of Dubai.
Article 16(1)(c) of the Employment Law 2019 requires an Employer to keep records of the Employee's Remuneration (gross and net, where applicable), and the applicable Pay Period.

The specific application described in case CFI 016/2025, the Claimant's Application No. CFI-016-2025/3 seeking a variation of the Judgment in relation to awarded costs (the 'No Costs Application'), was dismissed.

According to Article 12(4) of the Common Reporting Standard Law, Reporting Financial Institutions must retain records in an electronically readable format for a retention period of six (6) years after the date of reporting the information. The provided context for the General Partnership Law does not contain an Article 18(2)(b) specifying a retention period for records.

The application heard in case CFI 067/2025 was the Defendant's Application No. CFI-067-2025/1 dated 4 August 2025, which sought immediate judgment pursuant to Rule 24.1 of the DIFC Courts Rules and/or strike out pursuant to RDC 4.16. This application was dismissed by the Order of H.E. Justice Shamlan Al Sawalehi dated 26 January 2026.
Upon request, the Reporting Financial Institution must provide an English translation of the records to the Relevant Authority.
The DIFC Personal Property Law 2005 was enacted on the date specified in the Enactment Notice in respect of this Law, as the exact calendar date is not provided in the given context.
The Permission to Appeal Application was refused.

No, the question of whether Miranda rights were properly administered is not addressed in the provided context, as the documents pertain to civil proceedings in the DIFC Courts regarding the enforcement of an arbitral award and the collateral use of disclosed materials, not to criminal procedures or the administration of Miranda rights.
The consolidated version of the Law on the Application of Civil and Commercial Laws in the DIFC was published in November 2024.

The court's final ruling in case CFI 010/2024, as stated on the first page of the document, is that the Claimant's Application for a Default Costs Certificate is dismissed and the Claimant shall bear its own costs of the Application.
Confirmation Statement

The outcome of the specific order or application described in case SCT 295/2025 is not explicitly stated in the provided context. The context only describes the procedural background, including the review of the Judgment, the Claimant's Application for Permission to Appeal, the Defendant's response, and a hearing, but does not state the final decision on that application.

The Court of Appeal allowed the appeal in part, restoring the amended Second Part 50 order against Ms. Onita and dismissing the appeal as it related to Mr. Oron. The court found the judge was wrong in his approach and interpretation of the rule regarding Ms. Onita. On costs, the court ruled the appellant was in principle entitled to its costs but with a discount, as it succeeded substantially against Ms. Onita but failed against the other target (Mr. Oron). The appellant's claimed costs were USD 121,082.50, and the respondents' were USD 38,000, with the court noting a substantial disparity partly justified by the appellant's extensive research.

A DIFC-incorporated Limited Liability Partnership must preserve its accounting records for at least six years from the date they were created, unless a different period is prescribed in the Regulations. The context does not provide information regarding the preservation period for accounting records of a DIFC-incorporated General Partnership.
The context does not mention a jury decision in case ENF 269/2023; the document shows it was an enforcement order issued by H.E. Chief Justice Wayne Martin of the DIFC Court of Appeal.

The court decided to discharge the anti-suit injunction order with immediate effect, granted the Defendant's application to set it aside, and ordered the Claimant to pay the Defendant's costs of that application on the standard basis.
According to the 'IT IS HEREBY ORDERED THAT' section of case SCT 454/2024, the application for permission to appeal was refused.

No, there is no information about parole hearings in case CFI 057/2025. The provided context discusses court hearings related to injunctions, evidence submissions, and a permission to appeal application, but does not mention any parole hearings.
The DIFCA is responsible for administering the Employment Law and any Regulations made under it.
The Foundations Law is administered by the Registrar.
The purpose of Article 9(1) of the Foundations Law is to authorize the Board of Directors of the DIFCA to make Regulations to facilitate the administration of, or further the objects of, this Law.
